picks.game: Boston Celtics vs Philadelphia 76ers
picks.rationale: The Boston Celtics have a strong track record as favorites, covering the spread in 11 of their last 17 games when favored by 5 or more points. The Philadelphia 76ers, conversely, have struggled as home underdogs, going 2-10 ATS and SU in that situation since November 19. Boston also demonstrates excellent bounce-back ability, not having lost consecutive games since March 12, which is crucial after their Game 2 defeat. With Joel Embiid still sidelined for Game 3, the 76ers are without their primary offensive weapon. The Celtics' overall team strength and historical performance in similar spots make them a confident pick to cover this spread.

picks.game: Los Angeles Lakers vs Houston Rockets
picks.rationale: The Los Angeles Lakers enter Game 3 with a commanding 2-0 series lead and have consistently covered the spread against the Rockets, doing so in their last four head-to-head matchups. The line movement for this game saw Houston open at -9.5 and settle at -8.5, indicating that bettors are favoring the Lakers with the points. Houston's offense has been largely inefficient this season and has been held under 100 points in five of the last seven meetings with the Lakers. Despite key injuries to Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves, the Lakers have maintained strong defensive execution and received significant contributions from role players. This suggests the Lakers can keep the game close, even on the road.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Portland Trail Blazers
picks.rationale: The projected total for this game is 230.5, a substantial 10 points higher than the current line of 219.5, indicating a strong lean towards the Over. The uncertainty surrounding Victor Wembanyama's status after a head injury in Game 2 is likely suppressing the total, but both teams possess significant offensive firepower. Portland's offense, featuring Deni Avdija, Shaedon Sharpe, and Jerami Grant, is capable of scoring in bunches, and their league-leading second-chance points further boost scoring opportunities. The expected fast pace and transition-heavy nature of this playoff game also favor a higher-scoring outcome.

picks.game: Los Angeles Lakers vs Houston Rockets
picks.rationale: Both previous games in this series have gone under the total, establishing a clear trend for lower-scoring contests. The Houston Rockets have struggled with offensive inefficiency throughout the season and play at a slower pace, contributing to suppressed scoring. The Los Angeles Lakers' defense has been particularly effective in this series, consistently limiting Houston's offensive output. Public betting data shows a significant lean towards the Under, with 95% of public money on this side. The projected final score of Rockets 104, Lakers 99, totals 203 points, comfortably below the 206.5 line.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Portland Trail Blazers
picks.rationale: The Portland Trail Blazers carry momentum into Game 3 after stealing Game 2 in San Antonio, tying the series at 1-1. A critical factor is the questionable status of Victor Wembanyama for the Spurs due to a head injury, which significantly weakens San Antonio. Portland boasts a strong home record and has covered the spread in their last five home games against Western Conference opponents after winning as underdogs. With players like Scoot Henderson stepping up in Game 2 and a strong offensive rebounding advantage, the Blazers are well-equipped to keep this game close or even secure a home victory.

picks.game: Tampa Bay Lightning @ Montreal Canadiens
picks.rationale: The series between the Lightning and Canadiens has seen two one-goal overtime results, indicating offensive potential despite tight margins. While the Under has drawn 90% public support on the 5.5 total, creating a contrarian opportunity, both teams have shown high scoring rates during the regular season. Montreal is dealing with significant defensive injuries, with Noah Dobson out for the season and Mike Matheson missing practice, which could lead to more scoring chances for Tampa Bay's potent offense. The Lightning also hold a slight edge in scoring depth and goaltending reliability, reinforcing the expectation of a higher-scoring affair.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights @ Utah Mammoth
picks.rationale: The series is tied 1-1, but Vegas possesses strong playoff pedigree and a superior power-play profile, ranking 6th in power-play goals during the regular season. Despite Utah having home-ice advantage for Game 3 and a passionate crowd, Vegas has demonstrated mental toughness by bouncing back after their Game 2 loss. The betting market has seen public money heavily on Vegas, firming up the line in their direction, indicating sharp support even on the road. Vegas also holds a winning all-time record against Utah at 4-3.

picks.game: Edmonton Oilers @ Anaheim Ducks
picks.rationale: The Oilers are a formidable offensive team, ranking 6th in goals and 2nd in power-play goals during the regular season, and their power play is due to convert after an 0-for-4 performance in Game 2. Anaheim is contending with injuries to Radko Gudas and Ross Johnston, and their recent form is poor, with a 2-6-2 record in their last 10 games and failing to cover the puck line in 8 of those. The line has moved steadily towards Edmonton, with overwhelming public money on the Oilers, suggesting strong confidence in their ability to reassert control in the series. This situational edge, combined with Edmonton's offensive firepower, makes the -1.5 spread attractive.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights @ Utah Mammoth
picks.rationale: Mark Stone has proven to be a 'big game player' for the Golden Knights, scoring in both Game 1 and Game 2 of this series. He concluded the regular season strongly with five goals in a five-game span, indicating his current scoring touch. Stone is confirmed to be active and on the Vegas Golden Knights roster for this pivotal Game 3. Given Vegas's playoff experience and Stone's recent goal-scoring surge, he is a prime candidate to find the back of the net again.

picks.game: Edmonton Oilers @ Anaheim Ducks
picks.rationale: Both Game 1 and Game 2 of this series have been high-scoring affairs, with Edmonton winning 4-3 and Anaheim winning 6-4, demonstrating offensive capabilities from both sides. The Oilers possess an elite power play that is expected to convert after a quiet Game 2, and Anaheim has shown significant finishing upside. Public betting has heavily favored the Over at 60%, and Anaheim's goaltending, particularly Connor Ingram, has been identified as a concern, potentially leading to more goals. The Ducks' recent home struggles, losing five of their last six, also suggest defensive vulnerabilities that could contribute to a high-scoring game.

picks.game: Philadelphia Phillies vs Atlanta Braves
picks.rationale: The Atlanta Braves are in excellent form, boasting an 8-2 record in their last 10 games, while the Philadelphia Phillies are struggling with a nine-game losing streak. Atlanta has also dominated recent head-to-head matchups, winning four of the last five against Philadelphia. The line movement shows sharp money favoring the Braves, with their moneyline climbing from -136 to -149. The projected final score of Braves 7, Phillies 3 further supports Atlanta covering the -1.5 spread.

picks.game: Pittsburgh Pirates vs Milwaukee Brewers
picks.rationale: This game features a premier pitching matchup between Pittsburgh's Paul Skenes and Milwaukee's Brandon Woodruff, both of whom have impressive WHIPs under 1.00 and low ERAs. The total opened at 7 and has remained stable, with 100% of public money heavily backing the Under, indicating strong sharp agreement. The projected final score of Pirates 3, Brewers 2 reinforces the expectation of a low-scoring contest, making the Under 7 a high-confidence play.

picks.game: Minnesota Twins vs Tampa Bay Rays
picks.rationale: Significant reverse line movement indicates sharp action favoring the Tampa Bay Rays, with their moneyline moving from -116 to -122 despite public support. The Rays also hold a recent head-to-head advantage over the Twins. With Drew Rasmussen starting for Tampa Bay, who has posted strong early-season numbers and an elite WHIP, the Rays possess a clear pitching edge in what is projected to be a tight, low-scoring game.

picks.game: Cleveland Guardians vs Toronto Blue Jays
picks.rationale: The Cleveland Guardians are in a favorable position facing a Toronto Blue Jays team with several key pitching injuries, including George Springer, José Berrios, Yimi Garcia, and Shane Bieber on the IL. Cleveland's starting pitcher, Gavin Williams, has been exceptional with a 3-1 record and a 2.12 ERA, contrasting sharply with Toronto's starter, Max Scherzer, who holds a 7.16 ERA. The Guardians also boast a more productive offense recently, having hit 11 home runs in their last 10 games compared to Toronto's 5.

picks.game: Chicago Cubs vs Los Angeles Dodgers
picks.rationale: The Chicago Cubs are on an impressive hot streak, winning 9 of their last 10 games, and have demonstrated significant historical dominance over the Dodgers with a perfect 5-0 record in their last five head-to-head matchups. The Los Angeles Dodgers are dealing with several key injuries, including Blake Snell, Tommy Edman, and Mookie Betts, who is day-to-day. Given the Cubs' recent form and their strong head-to-head advantage against an injured Dodgers squad, the Cubs as underdogs at +140 present a high-value opportunity.

picks.game: Atlanta United FC @ Toronto FC
picks.rationale: Atlanta United is in very poor form this season with a 1-1-7 record and sits 29th in MLS, including an abysmal 0-0-3 away record. They are also battling numerous injuries, with key players like Miguel Angel Almiron Rejala (knee) and Steven Alzate (adductor) expected to be out. Toronto FC, despite their own injuries, has a better record at 3-2-4 and is playing at home, where Atlanta has struggled significantly.

picks.game: New York City FC @ CF Montreal
picks.rationale: CF Montreal has shown strong recent form, securing a 4-1 victory against New York Red Bulls in their last outing. Historically, Montreal has dominated this fixture, winning the previous three head-to-head matchups against New York City FC. New York City FC is coming off a demanding 4-4 draw just two days prior, which could significantly impact their energy levels for this away fixture.

picks.game: Los Angeles FC @ Minnesota United FC
picks.rationale: While LAFC has shown some defensive solidity, their attack remains potent, with Denis Bouanga scoring 6 goals and Son Heung-Min contributing 7 assists in recent matches. Minnesota United is on a four-game winning streak and has been scoring consistently, led by Kelvin Yeboah with five goals this season. Both teams possess strong offensive capabilities, suggesting an open and high-scoring contest.

picks.game: New York Red Bulls @ FC Cincinnati
picks.rationale: FC Cincinnati has been involved in high-scoring affairs, most notably their recent 4-4 draw against New York City FC just two days ago. Their attack, featuring Evander and Kévin Denkey, has proven capable of producing multiple goals. Given the quick turnaround and potential defensive vulnerabilities from both sides, this match is primed for another high-scoring outcome.

picks.game: New England Revolution @ Inter Miami CF
picks.rationale: Inter Miami CF holds a significant historical advantage over the New England Revolution, having won their last four head-to-head matchups, including a decisive 4-1 victory. The Revolution are facing a challenging schedule, playing their second away game in a short period after competing on April 22, which could lead to fatigue. Inter Miami will also benefit from playing at their new home stadium, Nu Stadium, likely with strong crowd support.
